what_it_does: This file is a completed noticeboard request that records a design decision and implementation summary for making clone reflections durable and addressable as first-class rows via a clone:// addressing scheme.
observations: State is done and the request progressed through open, picked-up, building, and done in about five minutes.
The title proposes clone:// fleet-addressing plus reflection-persistence so clones become first-class addressed rows.

The body cites consensus g-1781505586 and Tim's A+B unification, then lists three implementation pieces: a falsify-first bug-fix to persist reflections, a clone://<source_sid>/<at> address format, and consumer-paired lookup/recollection support.
The done transition note references commit 0bdeac3, a falsify-first 7/7 result, 19/19 regression pass, and declares reflections are now durable and addressed.
The address grammar uses compact:N, uuid:<uuid>, or ts:<iso> and is described as address-safe verbatim with no slugify needed.
It distinguishes clone:// from mind://, placing clone:// on the fleet/provenance axis.
standouts: The core fix is a silent-loss bug: onboard_clone returned a reflection but never wrote it to .data/clones/<h>.json.
It introduces clone://<source_sid>/<at> as a provenance-stable address for clones.
It treats the clone as one addressed row spanning two handle-files (.data/clones provenance + .data/channels membership) rather than adding a third registry.
It pairs a point-lookup consumer (get_by_address) with a semantic recollection consumer.
conventions: Follows the repository's YAML-frontmatter noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, timestamps, history, and links.
Uses board:// and clone:// URI-style identifiers.
Uses a ticket lifecycle with states open, picked-up, building, done and transition notes.
The markdown body is a concise design/implementation narrative rather than code.
concerns: The title is split across two lines in the YAML, making it slightly awkward to read.
The history note is dense and mixes commit hash, test counts, and feature summary.
Several terms (cc_clone lane, handle-files, Tim's A+B unification, mind://) are referenced without being defined in this file.
The clone:// grammar is only sketched; exact parsing and normalization rules are not shown.
notes: A done request ticket that documents how clone reflections were made durable and addressable through a new clone:// scheme and a falsify-first bug fix.
questions: What exactly is the cc_clone lane and how does it interact with the two handle-files?
What is Tim's A+B unification and what do A and B represent?
What is the full data schema of .data/clones/<h>.json after the reflection field is added?
How is clone://<source_sid>/<at> parsed and validated, and what does compact:N specifically mean?
What is the mind:// scheme and how does it differ in practice from clone://?
Which components implement get_by_address and recollection, and how do they ingest the reflection?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to implement clone:// fleet-addressing and reflection-persistence; the file records that this was completed, including the bug fix, addressing scheme, and consumer integration, so purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Unification of clone state across files
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to eliminate silent data loss for clone-generated reflections and to elevate clones into the same addressed-state model as other first-class entities, enabling both direct lookup and semantic recall.
novelty: The clone://<source_sid>/<at> provenance-stable address and the treatment of a clone as a single addressed row formed by joining two existing handle-files, avoiding a separate registry.
contribution: This file contributes a design record and implementation summary for persisting clone reflections, a clone:// URI addressing convention, and a consumer pairing pattern that links point lookup with semantic recollection.
summary_for_embedding: A completed noticeboard request documenting the design and implementation of clone:// fleet addressing and reflection persistence so that clone reflections become durable, first-class addressed rows.
intention_for_embedding: To fix the silent loss of clone reflections and establish a provenance-stable addressing scheme for clones with integrated lookup and recollection consumers.
